goal: Implement Aider repo-map reimplementation in Rust per master-plan §1.1 + §3.5 + §4.5 + §18 Phase 3 Week 10 line 1808 — a NEW `crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs` module that runs personalized PageRank over the knowledge-graph's `kind = "calls"` relation edges, applies a 50× relevance bias to recently-queried files via the personalization vector, and fits the resulting symbol list into a configurable token budget. This is the highest-leverage solo unblocker for the remaining Phase 3 backlog: F01 unlocks F04 (G5 Context query), F09 (Quality-maximalist response assembly), F11 (bonus context selector), AND F16 (full query-pipeline integration suite). attempts=0; first-attempt clean expected.
plan_summary: Solo-feature P3-W10-F01 implementation WO. NEW `crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs` module + `mod context_compiler;` declaration appended to `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs`. Inside `context_compiler.rs`: (a) public `RepoMap` struct holding the ranked + budget-fitted symbol list with per-symbol PageRank score (`score: f64`) + estimated token count + the source `Entity` projection; (b) public `RepoMapOptions` struct with `damping: f64 = 0.85`, `max_iterations: usize = 100`, `tolerance: f64 = 1e-6`, `recency_bias_multiplier: f64 = 50.0`, `token_budget: usize` (caller-supplied — 8000 typical); (c) public `build_repo_map(kg: &KnowledgeGraph, recently_queried_files: &HashSet<PathBuf>, options: &RepoMapOptions) -> Result<RepoMap, RepoMapError>` entry point (see scope_in #4 for KG enumeration strategy); (d) hand-rolled sparse personalized-PageRank iteration over `(entity_id, entity_id)` adjacency derived from `kind = "calls"` Relations (NO new deps — `ndarray` workspace dep is available but unnecessary for sparse iteration); (e) token-counting heuristic via `entity_token_estimate(entity)` returning `entity.signature.len()/4 + entity.doc_comment.len()/4 + entity.qualified_name.len()/4 + 8` (≈ 4 chars / token, the standard tiktoken-cl100k_base lower-bound — documented inline). Frozen module-root unit test `test_repo_map_pagerank` (DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement, NOT inside `mod tests { ... }` — substring selector `context_compiler::test_repo_map_pagerank` resolves cleanly per WO-0070 lessons). The test seeds an in-memory KG (rusqlite `:memory:` via `KnowledgeGraph::open_in_memory` if available OR via `tempfile::tempdir` + a knowledge.db path), inserts 6 entities + 6+ `calls` relations forming a deliberately-asymmetric DAG with a known PageRank-rank order under no recency bias, then re-runs WITH `recently_queried_files = {file_b}` to assert the 50× bias flips file_b's symbols above the structural-PageRank winner. Token-budget fit assertion: with a small `token_budget = 30`, the returned `RepoMap.symbols` MUST be a strict prefix of the unbudgeted ranking AND MUST sum-of-token-estimates ≤ 30. Mutation contract: M1 (remove 50× bias multiplier — the test's recency-bias-flip assertion fails); M2 (remove token-budget fit — the test's budget-fit assertion fails); M3 (negate the PageRank update equation — the test's structural-rank assertion fails). All three mutations reversible via `git checkout --` + md5sum verify. Estimated 4-5 commits: (1) module skeleton + RepoMapOptions + RepoMap structs + lib.rs mod-decl; (2) PageRank iteration loop + KG enumeration; (3) token-budget fitting + 50× recency bias personalization; (4) test_repo_map_pagerank with seeded KG + 3 mutation-targeted assertions; (5) RFR.
scope_in: 1. Create NEW `crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs` (file does NOT exist at planner-time — verified via `! test -e crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs`). Module rustdoc preamble (40-60 lines) cites: master-plan §1.1 line 44 (PageRank-based deep-fusion), §3.5 (G5 Context group), §4.5 line 345 (Aider repo-map (reimplemented) — Library (internal, Rust), P0), §6.1 line 506 (Aider-style repo-map, PageRank, 50x bias toward relevant files), §6.3 line 660 (Response assembly — full detail for relevant content), §18 Phase 3 Week 10 line 1808 (Aider repo-map reimplementation in Rust + Context7 + Repomix), §17.2 line 1634 (`context_compiler.rs` listed in the canonical ucil-core layout), DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), the workspace's existing `chrono`/`thiserror`/`tracing`/`rusqlite` deps. The preamble MUST explicitly state: (a) input is the §12.1 entities + relations tables; (b) algorithm is hand-rolled personalized PageRank with damping=0.85, max_iter=100, tol=1e-6 (workspace-canonical defaults); (c) recency-bias multiplier is 50.0 per master-plan §6.1 line 506; (d) token-budget fitting uses a 4-char-per-token heuristic (cl100k_base lower-bound) and is a structural budget (caller may multiply by an external tokenizer ratio if needed); (e) NO new dependencies — sparse PageRank uses `HashMap<i64, Vec<i64>>` adjacency derived from `KnowledgeGraph::list_relations_by_source` filtered to `kind == "calls"`.

2. Define public types (each with full rustdoc citing the master-plan section and a `# Examples` block where non-trivial — per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Docs):
    (a) `pub struct RepoMapOptions { pub damping: f64, pub max_iterations: usize, pub tolerance: f64, pub recency_bias_multiplier: f64, pub token_budget: usize }` with `Default` impl returning `damping=0.85, max_iterations=100, tolerance=1e-6, recency_bias_multiplier=50.0, token_budget=8000` (the master-plan §6.1 + §6.3 defaults; documented inline).
    (b) `pub struct RankedSymbol { pub entity: Entity, pub score: f64, pub token_estimate: usize }` — one ranked entry; `entity` is the §12.1 row projection from `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs::Entity`.
    (c) `pub struct RepoMap { pub symbols: Vec<RankedSymbol>, pub total_tokens: usize, pub iterations: usize, pub converged: bool }` — `symbols` is sorted by `score` descending then by `entity.qualified_name` ascending (deterministic tie-break); `total_tokens = symbols.iter().map(|s| s.token_estimate).sum()`; `iterations` is the actual iteration count when convergence reached OR `max_iterations` if not; `converged = iterations < max_iterations`.
    (d) `#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)] #[non_exhaustive] pub enum RepoMapError { #[error("knowledge graph access: {0}")] KnowledgeGraph(#[from] KnowledgeGraphError), #[error("empty graph: no `calls` relations and no entities")] EmptyGraph }` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors (libraries: thiserror + a single Error enum per crate, #[non_exhaustive]).

3. Implement the algorithm as TWO public functions PLUS one internal helper:
    (a) `pub fn build_repo_map(kg: &KnowledgeGraph, recently_queried_files: &HashSet<PathBuf>, options: &RepoMapOptions) -> Result<RepoMap, RepoMapError>` — the entry point. Reads ALL entities from the KG (see #4), reads ALL `kind == "calls"` relations (see #4), constructs the personalization vector (entries for files in `recently_queried_files` get `recency_bias_multiplier × 1/N` mass; other entries get `1/N` mass; renormalized to sum to 1.0), calls `personalized_page_rank`, sorts the `(entity_id → score)` map descending by score (then by `qualified_name` ascending for tie-break), then calls `fit_to_budget`.
    (b) `pub fn personalized_page_rank(adjacency: &HashMap<i64, Vec<i64>>, personalization: &HashMap<i64, f64>, options: &RepoMapOptions) -> (HashMap<i64, f64>, usize, bool)` — pure function (no KG/IO dependency); takes a sparse adjacency `node_id → Vec<outgoing_neighbor_ids>`, the personalization vector (must sum to 1.0; renormalized internally if not), the options; returns `(score_map, iterations, converged)`. PageRank update rule: `score_new[v] = (1-d) * pers[v] + d * Σ (score_old[u] / out_degree(u)) for u in incoming(v)`. Convergence: L1 norm of `(score_new - score_old)` < tolerance. Dangling-node handling: nodes with no outgoing edges distribute their score uniformly across all nodes (the standard 'teleport' treatment). Includes `#[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(adjacency, personalization), fields(num_nodes = adjacency.len(), max_iter = options.max_iterations), name = "ucil.core.context_compiler.page_rank")]` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Tracing + master-plan §15.2.
    (c) `fn fit_to_budget(ranked: Vec<RankedSymbol>, token_budget: usize) -> (Vec<RankedSymbol>, usize)` — returns the strict prefix of `ranked` whose cumulative `token_estimate` does not exceed `token_budget`, plus the cumulative tokens. Greedy prefix-fit (NOT knapsack — the master-plan calls for a 'fits symbol list into a configurable token budget' which is the standard Aider repo-map prefix-greedy semantic at top-of-rank).

4. KG enumeration strategy (the existing KG API has `list_entities_by_file`, `list_relations_by_source`, `list_relations_by_target` but no `list_all_entities` / `list_relations_by_kind`). Two acceptable enumeration paths:
    (a) **PREFERRED**: Add a NEW `pub fn list_all_calls_relations(&self) -> Result<Vec<Relation>, KnowledgeGraphError>` AND `pub fn list_all_entities(&self) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` to `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` (additive, ~30 LOC each). Both queries are simple `SELECT ... FROM <table> [WHERE kind = ?1]` with full-column projection mirroring the existing `list_entities_by_file` / `list_relations_by_source` patterns; both include `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotations matching the existing tracing conventions in `knowledge_graph.rs`. Document inline (rustdoc) that these are full-table scans intended for the repo-map's one-shot warm-up phase, NOT hot-loop callers; cite master-plan §3.5 + §4.5 + WO-0087.
    (b) **ALTERNATE** (if executor finds full-table scans cause a coverage regression OR a clippy lint): use `KnowledgeGraph::execute_in_transaction` + raw `prepare("SELECT ...")` inside `build_repo_map` to scope the SQL to the function. Document in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section per WO-0070/0083/0084 spirit-over-literal precedent. The PREFERRED path is recommended because the new methods are reusable for downstream G5/G3/G4 wirings.
    Either path: NEVER edit the schema; NEVER add a `WHERE kind = 'calls'` index (master-plan §12.1 + the existing `INIT_SQL` schema is the load-bearing source of truth — only the `relations` table's existing indexes matter).

5. Personalization vector construction (master-plan §6.1 line 506 'PageRank, 50x bias toward relevant files'): given the full set `entities`, the file path `e.file_path` for each entity `e`, and the input `recently_queried_files: HashSet<PathBuf>`: (a) for each entity whose `PathBuf::from(&entity.file_path)` is contained in `recently_queried_files`, set `personalization[entity.id] = recency_bias_multiplier * (1.0 / N)` where `N = entities.len()`; (b) for all other entities, set `personalization[entity.id] = 1.0 / N`; (c) renormalize: `personalization[v] /= sum(personalization)` for all `v` so the vector sums to 1.0. Edge case: if `recently_queried_files` is empty, the personalization vector is uniform (1/N) and PageRank reduces to the unbiased centrality ranking — document inline. Edge case: if EVERY entity's file is in `recently_queried_files`, the multiplier cancels out via the renormalization (multiplying every entry by 50 then renormalizing yields the uniform vector) — document inline as `the bias is a relative signal, not an absolute one`. Path comparison MUST be `PathBuf` equality (`PathBuf::from(file_path)` constructed once per entity); MUST NOT do string-suffix matching (a `recently_queried_files` containing `"src/foo.rs"` is intentionally NOT a match for an entity with `file_path = "vendor/dep/src/foo.rs"`).

6. Token-counting heuristic. The function `fn entity_token_estimate(entity: &Entity) -> usize` returns `(entity.signature.as_deref().unwrap_or("").chars().count() + entity.doc_comment.as_deref().unwrap_or("").chars().count() + entity.qualified_name.as_deref().unwrap_or(&entity.name).chars().count()) / 4 + 8`. The `+ 8` constant is a per-symbol overhead for newlines + delimiters in the rendered repo-map line (documented inline). The `/4` is the cl100k_base lower-bound (1 token ≈ 4 chars for ASCII source; this is the same heuristic Aider's original uses for budget-fitting before invoking tiktoken). Document inline the heuristic's caveats (CJK / dense-token sequences will under-count; a future ADR may swap in `tiktoken-rs` for production but the WO-0087 acceptance test does NOT depend on cl100k_base accuracy — only on the budget being respected on the known fixture). MUST use `chars().count()` not `len()` (the latter returns bytes, biasing the heuristic toward overcounts on multi-byte source).

7. The unit test `test_repo_map_pagerank` MUST live at MODULE ROOT of `context_compiler.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement + WO-0070 lesson §`pub async fn test_g3_parallel_merge` lives at `executor.rs:3167` (NOT under `mod tests { ... }`); substring selector `executor::test_g3_parallel_merge` resolves cleanly`. Write as `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_repo_map_pagerank() { ... }`. The selector `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_repo_map_pagerank` MUST resolve uniquely without `--exact` (substring match — the `tests::` infix would break this; module-root placement is mandatory). Pre-flight: `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_repo_map_pagerank --list 2>&1 | grep -c 'test_repo_map_pagerank: test'` returns exactly 1.

8. Test body construction. Use `tempfile::tempdir()` + `KnowledgeGraph::open(&tmpdir.path().join("knowledge.db"))` to seed an isolated KG (no shared state with other tests). Insert 6 entities forming a directed-acyclic call graph with a known structural-PageRank winner: e.g., `qualified_name = "a::root"` (file_a.rs), `"a::child1"` (file_a.rs), `"a::child2"` (file_a.rs), `"b::handler"` (file_b.rs), `"b::helper"` (file_b.rs), `"c::leaf"` (file_c.rs). Insert 6+ `kind = "calls"` Relations: e.g., `root → child1`, `root → child2`, `child1 → handler`, `child2 → handler`, `handler → helper`, `helper → leaf`. Under unbiased PageRank, `handler` accumulates the most score (it has 2 incoming edges from high-PageRank `child1`+`child2` PLUS 1 outgoing edge to `helper`; `helper` is downstream and `leaf` is the sink). Three load-bearing assertions (each with SA-tagged operator-readable panic message per WO-0042 + WO-0085 §verifier `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` pattern):
    (SA1) **Structural PageRank ranking**: with `recently_queried_files = HashSet::new()` (empty — uniform personalization), call `build_repo_map(&kg, &empty, &options_with_large_budget)` and assert the top-ranked symbol's `qualified_name` is `"b::handler"` (the structural winner). Panic body: `(SA1) structural pagerank winner expected b::handler; observed {top_qn:?}`. This assertion is targeted by M3 (PageRank-update-equation negation).
    (SA2) **50× recency bias flips the ranking**: with `recently_queried_files = HashSet::from([PathBuf::from("src/file_a.rs")])`, call `build_repo_map(&kg, &recent, &options_with_large_budget)` and assert the top-ranked symbol's `file_path` is `"src/file_a.rs"` (the personalization vector's 50× bias on file_a's three entities pushes one of `a::root`/`a::child1`/`a::child2` above `b::handler`). Panic body: `(SA2) recency-bias top symbol expected file_a.rs; observed file_path={top_fp:?}, qualified_name={top_qn:?}`. This assertion is targeted by M1 (recency-bias-multiplier removal).
    (SA3) **Token-budget fit**: with `RepoMapOptions { token_budget: 30, ..options }` (a small budget that truncates the 6-symbol list), call `build_repo_map(&kg, &empty, &small_budget)` and assert: (i) `result.total_tokens <= 30`, (ii) `result.symbols.len() < 6` (strict truncation occurred), (iii) the returned `result.symbols` is a strict-prefix subset of the full ranking returned with `token_budget = 100_000`. Panic bodies: `(SA3a) token budget exceeded: total_tokens={n} > 30`; `(SA3b) budget did not truncate: returned all {n} symbols`; `(SA3c) budgeted prefix mismatch: symbol[i].qualified_name={budgeted_qn} != unbudgeted[i].qualified_name={unbudgeted_qn}`. This triple-assertion is targeted by M2 (token-budget-fit removal).
    Test MUST run synchronously (no `#[tokio::test]`); MUST NOT touch the network; MUST clean up the tempdir via the `Drop` impl of `tempfile::TempDir`.

9. **Pre-bake 3 mutation entries.** Pattern carried from WO-0042 → WO-0086 (now 42+ WOs deep). Each mutation: in-place `Edit` patch + targeted cargo-test selector + `git checkout --` restore + md5sum verify.
    **M1 (recency-bias multiplier removal)**: Edit `crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs` to replace the recency-bias application — find `if recently_queried_files.contains(&path) { options.recency_bias_multiplier * uniform_mass } else { uniform_mass }` (or the equivalent literal source line) and change it to `uniform_mass` (always — bypasses the bias). Re-run `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_repo_map_pagerank`; MUST FAIL with the SA2 panic (`(SA2) recency-bias top symbol expected file_a.rs; observed file_path="src/file_b.rs", qualified_name="b::handler"`). Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs`.
    **M2 (token-budget fit removal)**: Edit `fit_to_budget` to return `(ranked, total)` unconditionally (skip the prefix-greedy truncation). Re-run; MUST FAIL with one of the SA3 panics (`(SA3a) token budget exceeded: total_tokens=N > 30` for some N>30, OR `(SA3b) budget did not truncate: returned all 6 symbols`). Restore via `git checkout --`.
    **M3 (PageRank update equation negation)**: Edit the personalized-PageRank update — replace `score_new[v] = (1.0 - d) * pers[v] + d * incoming_sum` with `score_new[v] = (1.0 - d) * pers[v] - d * incoming_sum` (sign flip on the second term). Re-run; MUST FAIL with the SA1 panic (`(SA1) structural pagerank winner expected b::handler; observed {<some other qn>}`). Restore.
    Each mutation ≤ 3 lines of source change, ≤ 60 seconds wall-time. Pre-stage a single md5 snapshot: `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs > /tmp/wo-0087-context-compiler-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation; confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0087-context-compiler-orig.md5` after each `git checkout --`. If `list_all_calls_relations` / `list_all_entities` are added to `knowledge_graph.rs` (path #4(a) PREFERRED), pre-stage `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs > /tmp/wo-0087-knowledge-graph-orig.md5` separately — those API additions are NOT mutated under M1/M2/M3 but MUST round-trip via md5sum to confirm zero accidental change.

10. **Module declaration in `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs`**. The file's existing `mod` declarations live at the top per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate-layout (`src/lib.rs` is **only** re-exports and module declarations — no logic). Add `pub mod context_compiler;` in the alphabetical spot (between `mod ceqp;` and `mod cross_group;` if alphabetical-sort holds; otherwise between the closest existing modules — verify by reading the existing `mod` block). Add public re-exports for the WO-0087-public types: `pub use context_compiler::{RepoMap, RepoMapOptions, RankedSymbol, RepoMapError};` mirroring the existing `pub use cross_group::{...};` shape. NO logic in `lib.rs`. Pre-flight: `grep -E '^(mod|pub mod) ' crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` after the edit must show `pub mod context_compiler;` exactly once.

The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` returns empty before first push. Per WO-0086 lessons §executor + WO-0048 line 363 carve-out, the test body's panic strings (e.g., `(SA1) structural pagerank winner expected b::handler; observed ...`) are NOT subject to word-ban scrutiny. The KG-API additions to `knowledge_graph.rs` (if path #4(a) selected) MUST also pass the word-ban grep.

Per planner-time read: `tempfile.workspace = true` IS already present in dev-deps; NO Cargo.toml change should be needed. NO new workspace deps; NO new external crates (the workspace's existing `chrono`/`thiserror`/`tracing`/`rusqlite` + `std::collections::HashMap` are sufficient).

Confirm cargo selector resolution PRE-PUSH: `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_repo_map_pagerank --list 2>&1 | grep -E 'test_repo_map_pagerank: test'` shows exactly one match. NO tracing on `build_repo_map` or `fit_to_budget` — the WO-0067 lessons §planner #2 carve-out for pure-deterministic helpers applies (PageRank's iteration is deterministic; budget-fitting is a pure prefix-greedy operation). The single `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotation is on the iterative kernel where convergence-or-not is the load-bearing observability axis. Per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Tracing + master-plan §15.2.

15. Errors: per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors — libraries use `thiserror` + a single `Error` enum per crate. The crate already has `KnowledgeGraphError` (per-module) — `RepoMapError` follows the SAME per-module pattern (one error enum per logical area; the `From<KnowledgeGraphError>` impl bridges them). NO `anyhow` (binary-only). NO `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors line 'Never .unwrap() / .expect() outside #[cfg(test)]. Use ? and a typed error.'. Test code MAY use `.expect("<reason>")` per the carve-out.

NO `pub use` re-exports beyond the four named in scope_in #10 (RepoMap, RepoMapOptions, RankedSymbol, RepoMapError) — per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate-layout `pub(crate)` is the default visibility; promote to `pub` only when a consumer needs it.

17. **§11 hot-cold tier — DOES NOT APPLY** to this WO (data-only algorithm + KG-read; no cache surfaces; no warm-tier promotion). The repo-map runs on-demand from cold-tier (the §12.1 entities + relations tables); future ADR may explore caching the PageRank vector across queries, but for THIS WO the PageRank is computed fresh per call. Cite WO-0067 §planner #2 + WO-0085 §planner pure-deterministic carve-out as precedent.

Mutations that produce a compile error OR pass the test silently are NOT acceptable mutation forms — alternate mutation forms targeting the same load-bearing line ARE acceptable per WO-0070/0083/0084 spirit-over-literal precedent, documented in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section.

24. Bundle-shape rationale (cite in commit body for the first feat commit): solo-feature implementation per WO-0086 §planner explicit recommendation. P3-W10-F01 is the highest-leverage solo unblocker (unblocks F04, F09, F11, F16). The implementation is module-internal (`crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs`) + a small additive lib.rs mod-decl + (optionally) two new KG read helpers — all within ucil-core. Estimated 4-5 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence. NO bundling with another feature: F13 (warm processors) is in `crates/ucil-core` but in a different submodule (warm tier scheduler), F11/F16 (G8 test discovery / quality pipeline integration test suite) are in different test directories, and F09/F11 require F01 to land first. F01-solo is the structurally correct shape.

25. Estimated commits 4-5 (per DEC-0005 module-coherence): (1) `feat(core): add context_compiler module skeleton with RepoMap + RepoMapOptions + RepoMapError types` (~120 LOC including rustdoc); (2) `feat(core): implement personalized PageRank iteration with sparse adjacency` (~100 LOC); (3) `feat(core): wire KG enumeration + 50× recency bias + token-budget fitting in build_repo_map` (~100 LOC); (4) `test(core): seed KG with 6-entity DAG and assert structural+recency+budget invariants` (~150 LOC including the test); (5) `docs(work-orders): WO-0087 ready-for-review` (RFR doc). The KG API additions to `knowledge_graph.rs` (path #4(a) PREFERRED) MAY be a separate commit ("feat(core): add list_all_entities + list_all_calls_relations helpers for repo-map") OR may be folded into commit #3 — DEC-0005 module-coherence absorbs either choice.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT wire `RepoMap` into the daemon's G5 `ContextSource` impl OR into the `executor::test_g5_context_assembly` cargo selector — those are P3-W10-F04 scope (`G5 (Context) parallel query`) which depends on F01 + F02 + F03 + P3-W9-F03. F04 will be a follow-up WO consuming the WO-0087 public types.

2. Do NOT introduce a real tokenizer (tiktoken-rs OR cl100k_base via tokenizers crate). The 4-char-per-token heuristic is sufficient for the WO-0087 acceptance test AND for the WO-0087-scope deliverable per master-plan §6.3 (the master plan does NOT mandate a specific tokenizer for the v0.1.0 release; a future ADR may swap in `tiktoken-rs` for higher-fidelity production budgeting). The heuristic IS load-bearing for the WO-0087 SA3 budget-fit assertion.

3. Do NOT modify the §12.1 entities OR relations schema. Do NOT add migration scripts. Do NOT add new SQLite indexes. The PageRank algorithm operates over the existing schema; full-table scans are acceptable for the v0.1.0 repo-map (a future ADR may add a `relations_by_kind` index if benchmarks show the scan dominates wall-clock at scale).

4. Do NOT add `petgraph` OR `nalgebra` OR `sprs` OR any other graph/sparse-linear-algebra crate to the workspace. The hand-rolled sparse PageRank iteration (~80 LOC) is preferred per the workspace philosophy of minimizing external deps; `ndarray` is already in the workspace but UNNECESSARY for the sparse case. NO new Cargo dependencies under this WO.

5. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` table. The `[dev-dependencies]` may stay as-is (tempfile + proptest already present). Pre-flight: `git diff main -- crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml | grep -E '^\+' | wc -l` returns 0.
6. Do NOT modify `tests/fixtures/**` (forbidden_paths). The unit test seeds an in-memory KG via `KnowledgeGraph::open(<tmpdir>/knowledge.db)`; NO fixture-on-disk dependency.

7. Do NOT alter `scripts/gate/**` or `scripts/flip-feature.sh` (forbidden_paths). NO new `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F01.sh` is needed — F01's acceptance test is a `cargo_test` (not a script) per `feature-list.json:1895-1900`.
8. Do NOT add new env-vars. The repo-map's options are passed via the `RepoMapOptions` struct; NO `UCIL_REPO_MAP_*` env-var is introduced under this WO.
9. Do NOT introduce `unsafe` blocks. The hand-rolled PageRank iteration is safe Rust over `HashMap<i64, f64>` + `HashMap<i64, Vec<i64>>` — no FFI, no transmute, no raw pointers.
10. Do NOT split the F01 deliverable across multiple feat-branches. ONE feat branch (`feat/WO-0087-aider-repo-map-pagerank`) per DEC-0005 module-coherence.

11. Do NOT add `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, OR `xfail` to `test_repo_map_pagerank`. The test is fully deterministic and fast (≤ 1s wall-time on the 6-entity graph). Per .claude/CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.
12. Do NOT add backwards-compat shims for the public `RepoMap` / `RepoMapOptions` types — this is the FIRST landing of these types, no consumers exist yet, no compat surface to preserve.

13. Do NOT introduce a separate benchmark file under `crates/ucil-core/benches/`. Benchmark coverage for the repo-map is OUT OF SCOPE for the WO-0087 scope (a future ADR may add a `criterion` bench when F04 production-wiring lands and the graph size becomes performance-critical).
14. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider, embedding-provider, OR network-touching surface. The repo-map is fully local + deterministic.
15. Do NOT introduce graph-DB or external-service sidecar provisioning. The repo-map operates over the existing SQLite knowledge graph at `.ucil/shared/knowledge.db` (or the test's tempdir copy).
16. Do NOT modify scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh OR scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh — the standing carry-forwards (42+ WOs deep) remain out-of-scope.
17. Verifier MUST NOT set any `UCIL_SKIP_*` envs (this WO has no skip gate; the test is purely deterministic; the algorithm runs in <100ms on the 6-entity test graph).
18. Do NOT add new entries to `feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.
19. Do NOT modify any plugin manifests under `plugins/**` — F01 is a core-library implementation, not a plugin.

20. Do NOT modify any tests under `crates/ucil-core/tests/**` — the WO-0087 unit test lives at module root of `context_compiler.rs` (not as an integration test). The cargo selector requires module-root placement per scope_in #7.
